title: DISTRIBUTE SRILA PRABHUPADA BOOKS | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: Hare Krishna. So today we will be talking about Srila Prabhupada's books and books marathon. Some
of you were there last year. How many of us were there last year when we talked about books
marathon? Okay. So Prabhupada started this movement. Anyone can help you with an answer?
Which year? 71. So Srila Prabhupada went to the West in the year 1965 and established formally
registered International Society for Krishna Consciousness in the year 1966. So when Srila
Prabhupada, his Prabhupada's Guru Maharaj had given him, why did he go to the West? His
spiritual master Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Thakur had ordered him that since you are,
you can speak English, you have got educated in an English medium, these things, preach
this message, spread this message in the Western world. Bhagavad Gita is not a sectarian message
only for Indians, only for Hindus. It's a universal message. So he says, preach this
message in the Western world. It's a very materialistic society. So to fulfill the order
of his Guru, Prabhupada went to the West. And many of us know he was 70 years old. He
had 40 rupees in his pocket. He had a lot of money in his pocket. He had a lot of money.
With great difficulty, he got a passage in a cargo ship. He went to US and one year he
was struggling, no proper place to stay. No idea how long he is going to continue in US.
And 1966, he formally registered International Society for Krishna Consciousness. And slowly
people started coming.
He started conducting Gita classes. And more and more people started, you know, coming
in touch with Krishna Consciousness and started becoming devotees. So Prabhupada, from the
very beginning, wanted to distribute this message to more and more people. And his Guru
Maharaj also had instructed that if you get
an opportunity, print and distribute books. They called the books as Bhrad Mridanga. Mridanga
means, we know Mridanga, you know, we play the sound during Kirtan. But the sound of
that Mridanga, if you play it, just goes in this hall. But Bhrad Mridanga means the sound,
the transcendental vibration which is there in the book can spread all over the world
through the medium of books.
So, his Guru Maharaj also ordered, if you get money, print and distribute books. So
that was his main, what you call, to distribute this, disseminate this message, Prabhupada
emphasised a lot on writing, publishing, printing and distributing books. Before going to the
West, Prabhupada, when he was a grahasta, he single handedly, with great difficulty,
he would go all the way to Delhi. He was staying in Vrindavan. So that was his main, what you
call, distribute this message. So, this is an example where you could say, you know,
in Vrindavan and he would print a magazine called Back to Godhead.
He wouldn't have even means to have proper clothes.
In that sense, he was financially quite challenged.
But in spite of that, he would go to the printer, sit with him, get the typeset done, single-handedly
print those Back to Godhead magazines and distribute them.
So when Prabhupada established ISKCON and the movement started growing, more and more
devotees joined, Prabhupada encouraged all of them to also distribute books.
If we have to reach this message to more and more people, one thing is, yes, we can all
gather together and we can speak, we can talk, we can give Bhagavad Gita discourse.
But another medium of disseminating this knowledge is through printing.
That's how Prabhupada encouraged many devotees to go and distribute the books.
So that's how in the initial days, 70s, many devotees were there in the airports, many
devotees were there in different malls and all that.
And during the December month, which happens to be a Christmas month in the Western countries,
especially it's unlike India, where you can see people walking around in roads and you
know, people are unlike in the West.
Most of the people are traveling.
In the cars only, you'll find them in malls and supermarkets.
And more so on Christmas time, people are having vacations.
So everyone would be in a festive mood and everyone would be in that sense accessible.
If you go in a mall, you invariably find people, you can access them, you can put a book table,
you can talk to them.
So book distribution was, you know, very...

We can't pay the debt, but we can pay the debt to some extent by serving the spiritual

being called all over the world as Prabhupada's Books Marathon.

none of them are making any money for their selfishness.

is very, very dear to me.

So we have Prabhupada's books, which are very, very authorized, very authentic.
It is very difficult for a common man to note the difference between a 24 karat gold, 18 karat gold or any imitation gold.
They all look alike, they glitter alike.
But only a goldsmith who has a good knowledge can discriminate and say that this is pure gold.
So Prabhupada's books in that sense are pure gold.

His objective is to give this knowledge to as many souls as possible.
